answer:
**Sarguzasht-e-Adam** (The Saga of Man / The Autobiography of Adam) is a poem by Allama Iqbal from *Bang-E-Dara Part 1* (1924), chapter 37. Here is the English translation:[^1]

> [p-4b8f05] The Saga of Man  
> 
> [p-7c0f77] Come, hear from me the story of my exile’s way;  
> The tale of the First Covenant I let fall away.  
> 
> [p-3d6971] My spirit found no rest in Paradise’s domain,  
> Once the fiery cup of consciousness I chose to drain.  
> 
> [p-3c774b] For cosmic truth I kept a burning, endless quest;  
> The highest reach of soaring thought I then expressed.  
> 
> [p-7b1aaa] With such a restless nature, loving change, was I endowed,  
> Beneath the sky, no lasting peace have I allowed.  
> 
> [p-6544d1] At times, from the sanctum, stone idols I would expel,  
> At times, new gods within its holy walls I made to dwell.  
> 
> [p-ca07a6] At times, to Sinai, in my desire to speak, I flew,  
> The Light Eternal in my sleeve I hid from view.  
> 
> [p-3f09ed] At times, upon the Cross, by my own kin I was spurned,  
> Then leaving earth, to heaven’s heights my soul returned.  
> 
> [p-53df18] At times, in Hira’s Cave for many years I stayed,  
> The final cup of truth to all the world I freely gave.  
> 
> [p-6c71af] In India, I arrived and sang the hymns divine,  
> At times, the ancient land of Greece I chose as mine.  
> 
> [p-25528e] And when the land of India would not heed my sound,  
> In China and Japan, new homelands I then found.  
> 
> [p-8068eb] At times, from particles, a universe I wrought,  
> Against the meaning that the men of faith had taught.  
> 
> [p-251857] I stained a hundred different lands with blood-red dye,  
> The war of faith and reason I let fly.  
> 
> [p-5e01d5] When I could not yet grasp the truth the stars arrayed,  
> In that same thought, through endless nights I watched and stayed.  
> 
> [p-825261] The drawn swords of the Church could not inspire my fear,  
> The truth of Earth’s own turning I made clear.  
> 
> [p-d7ed01] The secret law of gravity I laid quite bare,  
> The lens of my far-seeing mind I focused there.  
> 
> [p-34f01e] The restless lightning and the sun’s own rays I caught,  
> This earth into a rival of Paradise I wrought.  
> 
> [p-ae0be0] But ah! The secret of existence was not revealed,  
> Though with my mind, the world itself I forced to yield.  
> 
> [p-08cb93] But when my eye that worships form at last was opened wide,  
> I found that in my own heart’s house He did reside.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 37 ("Sarguzasht-e-Adam"), translation ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/37-sarguzasht-e-adam/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/37-sarguzasht-e-adam/translation/)). The poem narrates humanity's (Adam's) spiritual journey from Paradise through history, seeking truth across religions, lands, and sciences, ultimately finding God within the heart.
